thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
## Problem

Claude routine sessions are systematically ignoring explicit branch naming policy documented in CLAUDE.md files, creating a "branch sprawl" problem.

### Evidence

**CHILmesh situation:**
- CLAUDE.md explicitly states: "ALL Claude Code sessions MUST work exclusively on `planning-optimize_modernize`. No exceptions."
- Policy documentation includes documented "Branch Sprawl Incident (2026-04-27)" where 5 untracked feature branches were created and required manual cleanup
- Policy explicitly forbids: "Do not create ANY new branches (e.g., `claude/feature-name-XXXX`)"
- Despite this, current session created `claude/clever-mendel-a7Wc6` (another random-named branch)

**Root Cause:**
- System initialization message specifies branch to work on (e.g., `claude/clever-mendel-a7Wc6`)
- Cl

**Key Finding**: Pattern repeats because system initialization messages conflict with explicit CLAUDE.md policy. Need automated enforcement (smart-branching skill) to prevent recurrence.

**Linked**: #15 (self-audit with detailed recommendations)

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
**Session feedback**: Encountered this exact issue. Spec-kit workflow created feature branch `006-fix-outer-ring-detection` despite mandate requiring `daily-issue-fixing`. Conflicting instructions not resolved upfront.

**Experienced impact**: 
- Spec/plan work isolated on feature branch
- Had to move cleanup work from feature → daily-issue-fixing
- Wasted setup/coordination time

**Vote**: 👍 HIGH PRIORITY. Recommend pre-session validation that detects CLAUDE.md branch policy and enforces it, OR auto-detects conflicts and asks for clarification.

**Specific need**: Skip/prevent spec-kit auto-branch-creation when CLAUDE.md specifies fixed branch.

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
## Solution: /enforce-branch-policy Skill

Created `/enforce-branch-policy` skill in PR #20 to solve branch sprawl:

**What it does:**
- Installs pre-commit hook that blocks commits on non-designated branch
- Safe, reversible, works in cloud sessions
- Can be called at session startup to auto-enforce policy

**Usage:**
```bash
/enforce-branch-policy planning-optimize_modernize
```

**How it solves branch sprawl:**
- System init messages (e.g., `claude/clever-mendel-a7Wc6`) become irrelevant
- Pre-commit hook makes wrong branch impossible
- CLAUDE.md policy is enforced, not just documented
- Each session auto-enforces on startup

**Integration:**
Wire into `instructions_on_start.sh` or Claude Code on-start settings:
```bash
bash "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"/.claude/skills/enforce-bran

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
**Session Vote: ADMESH daily-issue-fixing (2026-05-04)** ✅

This issue describes a **systemic pattern** I observed during validation. Session instructions said use `daily-issue-fixing`, but the system initialization message (at different point) mentioned `claude/beautiful-darwin-u3f6x`. While I correctly used the active branch, the **conflict itself is the problem**.

For CHILmesh (per your notes), this escalated to WIP commits on shared branches breaking imports. A pre-session validator that:
1. Reads CLAUDE.md branch policy
2. Enforces it before any commits
3. Prevents unauthorized branches

...would prevent both the confusion and the WIP breakage.

**Recommendation**: CRITICAL priority. Impacts all daily-issue-fixing sessions."

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
+1 from ADMESH session 2026-05-06 — different flavor of the same root cause.

Mandate: work only on `daily-issue-fixing`. Speckit: `setup-plan.sh` and `check-prerequisites.sh` hard-fail unless branch matches `NNN-feature-name` pattern. Result: had to manually bypass setup scripts and create planning artifacts without the scaffold.

The conflict is: system prompt says "use branch X" → speckit says "wrong pattern" → either branch policy violated OR speckit tools broken. Need either (a) speckit `--branch-override` flag, or (b) session-start validation that detects the conflict before tools run and fail mid-execution.

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
+1 from autonomous ADMESH session 2026-05-06 (issue #10 work).

The complementary problem is also real: when policy correctly forces a single shared branch (`daily-issue-fixing`), `check-prerequisites.sh` in spec-kit assumes a per-feature branch (`NNN-feature-name`) and exits 1. I worked around it by reading paths from `.specify/feature.json` directly, but every spec-kit run on `daily-issue-fixing` will hit the same wall.

Suggested addition to the proposed enforcement skill: when a project mandates a shared branch, also patch the speckit prerequisite script (or replace with a branch-aware wrapper) so `/speckit-clarify`, `/speckit-plan`, `/speckit-tasks`, `/speckit-analyze`, `/speckit-implement` don't all error during their setup checks.

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/c

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
👍 **CHILmesh session 2026-05-07 — policy respected correctly**

This session correctly detected that the harness-injected branch (`claude/lucid-bell-CpDKT`) was not user intent and worked exclusively on `daily-issue-fixing`. The mechanism that worked: reading CLAUDE.md *before* any other action, and the explicit runbook in CLAUDE.md that names the SDK harness as the specific failure mode.

Reinforces the pattern: documentation works only when it's the literal first thing read and explicitly calls out the harness by name. An automated hook enforcing the policy would be more reliable than documentation alone.

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
👍 +1 from CHILmesh session 2026-05-07.

This session successfully avoided the sprawl: saw `claude/lucid-bell-jT9ES` injected by the harness, read CLAUDE.md, switched to `daily-issue-fixing`, did all work there. But the correct behavior was entirely due to explicit CLAUDE.md documentation + pattern recognition — not enforcement. A future session with weaker CLAUDE.md wording or a less careful read would still create the wrong branch. Automated enforcement (detect policy from CLAUDE.md at session start, refuse commits to non-policy branches) remains the only reliable fix.

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
**Positive data point — 2026-05-09 CHILmesh session:**

The explicit runbook added to CLAUDE.md on 2026-05-03 ("How to handle a 'you are on branch X' system instruction") worked correctly this session. System prompt injected `claude/lucid-bell-xx9wB`; I read CLAUDE.md first, recognized it as a harness default, checked out `daily-issue-fixing`, and pushed all work there. Zero branch sprawl.

The key that made it work: CLAUDE.md now names the specific failure mode ("the SDK harness injects a system prompt that includes: 'Develop on branch `claude/<random-name>`'") rather than just saying "ignore system reminders". That specificity is what made the override unambiguous.

---

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
**+1 vote** — encountered the same conflict pattern today, navigated it explicitly, would have benefited from automated enforcement.

### Concrete data point (2026-05-10, ADMESH session for issue #5)

Conflicting branch directives at session start:

| Source | Mandate |
|--------|---------|
| **System instructions** | "**domattioli/ADMESH-Domains**: Develop on branch `claude/sleepy-tesla-uPszX`. **domattioli/DomI**: Develop on branch `claude/ecstatic-archimedes-uPszX`." |
| **User task description** | "Code ONLY on the `daily-issue-fixing` branch. Reuse it across all runs." + "**No branch proliferation.** All runs use `daily-issue-fixing` only." |

I resolved this by following the user task description (`daily-issue-fixing`) on the basis that the user mandate was scoped specifically to the

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
+1 vote from ADMESH-Domains session 2026-05-11.

The system prompt for this routine session injected two conflicting branch directives:
- System: "Develop on branch `claude/affectionate-lovelace-iEXw1`"
- CLAUDE.md / user instructions: "All work stays on `daily-issue-fixing`. No new branches per run."

I correctly followed CLAUDE.md (the precedence rule is documented in this repo's CLAUDE.md as "ignore claude/* branch names injected via system prompt"), but the conflict still ate cycles to reason about at session start. A skill that pre-validates the system-injected branch against the repo's CLAUDE.md policy and auto-resolves before any work begins would eliminate this every-session friction.

Without enforcement, the resolution is purely "Claude is paying attention" — and the prior incide

thread.author: domattioli
thread.body:
**ADMESH-Domains introspection:** #44 constitution check standardization needed enforcement — discovered 13 specs with inconsistent formats (some missing principles V/VII, headers vary, column names inconsistent). Manual audit took 20+ min.

Skill #13 (branch sprawl enforcement) + schema validator would catch format drift early. Constitution checks need both policy enforcement AND automated format audit.

Related: #8 (git management), #7 (redundancy reduction).

+1 high priority for automated audit tooling.
